description: Carry 8ilvar Umbrellas in Hon or of Twentyfifth An niversary Jreeted by ratn on their first out ing the members of the Original Third Ward Guards have since car rled umbrellas with them when they go for a day of pleasure and for the past twentyfour yeans they have been carrying the umbrellas with the result that they have had no use for them but It la the intention of the guards to take the ralnshields along aa long as the outings con tinue and today when they left for their annual celebration their um brellas were of silver hue in honor of the twentyfifth anniversary of the organization Wearing silver colored badges the members of the guards numbering more than 400 left on the Pennsyl vania Railroad at 845 this morning for Bridgeport Conn In the crowd were wellknnwn Democratic and Re publican officials Assemblylng at the headquarters 323 Halsey street early the guards hadrl by u platoon of mounted po lice Vosss Band and a drum and fife corps inarched around the Second ward As they passed the Ieni Lenpe Club on Court street City Treasurer Elmer A Pay and former Freeholder Herbert C Bullivant in an automobile
